rulings.Case Title: JESSE BEVERLY JR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action For Delay In Prosecution
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, September 2, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Goodrich Corporation¿s motion to dismiss action for delay in prosecution is granted. No opposition filed. A hearing is required unless defense counsel informs plaintiff of the tentative ruling and plaintiff says that he does not contest the tentative ruling. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ROSS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, September 2, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Frank M. Booth, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment (issue 1) is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 2 (breach of duty and product identification) and taken off calendar as to issues 3-5 (strict liability, false representation and punitive damages) since those claims have been dismissed. Plaintiff¿s declaration creates a triable fact whether he was exposes to asbestos-containing materials attributable to defendant. Dr. Cohen¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether defendant knew or should have known of the health hazards of the asbestos-containing materials that it exposed Mr. Ross to. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ROSS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, September 2, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Turner Construction Company¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden that showing plaintiff does not posses and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that it knew or should reasonably have known of the health hazards of any asbestos-containing materials that it exposed Mr. Ross to and plaintiff failed to present any evidence creating a triable issue on that point. Plaintiff concedes the lack of merit of his strict liability and false representation claims. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ROSS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, September 2, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Colgate-Palmolive Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 1 (breach of duty) and granted as to issue 2 (punitive damages). Defendant failed to satisfy its burden of production that it did not know or should not reasonably have known that the asbestos-containing materials its employees exposed Mr. Ross to were hazardous. Defendant did not present any responses to contention interrogatories posed to plaintiff seeking this information. Plaintiff concedes that is punitive damages claim should be dismissed. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARCELL RATTLER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EHXIBITS et al

rulings.Case Title: MARCELL RATTLER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EHXIBITS et al

rulings.Case Title: STEVE DUTRA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, September 2, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Rexnord Industries, LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production due to defendant¿s failure to present responses to contention discovery on the issue of whether plaintiff was exposed to any asbestos-containing products attributable to defendant. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: EDDIE STEWART VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, September 2, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Lehigh Southwest Cement Company¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. The declarations of Randy George and Shellie Stewart create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to an asbestos-containing product (Monolith asbestos gun cement) attributable to defendant. The objections to those declarations are overruled. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production as to issues 2 and 3. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAN BUSBY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al

rulings.Case Title: RONALD SANDY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, September 2, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 13. Defendant Albay Construction Company¿s motion for summary judgment is granted and plaintiff¿s request for continuance is denied if Mr. Stainbrook¿s deposition did not take place on September 1 or, if it did take place, plaintiff cannot make a good faith representation that the deposition disclosed sufficient facts to enable him to oppose this motion. If by 4:30pm today an attorney for plaintiff sends an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org stating, without argument, that Mr. Stainbrook¿s deposition took place today and the deposition disclosed sufficient facts to enable plaintiff to oppose this motion, the motion will be continued to September 15, 2010 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 220, plaintiff will have until September 8 to file opposition papers and defendant will have until September 13 to file reply papers. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN DOBROCKE et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, September 2, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 16. Defendant ExxonMobil Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. There is a triable issue whether Mobill acted negligently due to its laborers disturbing asbestos-containing materials in Mr. Dobrocke¿s presence. The objections to the declaration of Mr. Nixt are overruled. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN DOBROCKE et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, September 2, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 16. Defendant Dowman Products, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternative motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 2 (false representation) and 4 (punitive damages) and denied as to issues 1 (strict liability) and 3 (loss of consortium). Plaintiffs¿ evidence creates a triable issue whether Mr. Dobrocke was exposed to asbestos-containing FixAll compound during his home remodel work. Plaintiffs failed to present any evidence creating a triable issue on their false representation and punitive damages claims. =(220/HEK)
